item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of feel the opposite, in that this is more wholesome than creepy. The entire thing is just a man who lives his daily life, and goes from that to a hero for Achebe. His actions are memorialized in this show, displaying that anyone can be a hero to another person.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This remains one of my favorite SCPs. Tight, concise writing with an interesting pay-off that leaves just enough mystery to be engaging. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He is the most important man to Achebe Okoro. Everything that Achebe does from this point on is completely due to him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Safety. Confinement. Protection.

No uncollapsed images on the forums, please. -Zyn
hide

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Clearly you don't know Television. Saving Achebe Okoro was clearly the "A" Plot of his life story. You don't end a series wrap with a character death with an unrelated asspull. His entire life was literally leading up to that moment.

The Foundation should detain Mr. Okoro and debrief him ASAP. Find out what he's doing in our reality that makes the guy who saved him The Most Important Man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I have seen a few SCPs that are television shows or just broadcasted in general, this one really pulls off a good unsettling vibe, which I love.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
to me that's part of what makes me like this so much.
no one is truly the most important man, and he's just as susceptible to esoteric nonsense as we are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was waiting for a pay-off, the "most important man" bit seemed like a foreshadowing, then he died and nothing much happened. The initial setup by itself is not very interesting to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, David is important to *himself* (most people are the main characters in their own life stories, after all). And, when he saw the tourist Achebe being mugged, he saw an opportunity to make himself into a hero.

Also, maybe he has an undiscovered power to create & project these videos. This is the anomaly here but it doesn't really matter now, does it? I like that idea.

In any case, he still died giving the ultimate gift to a stranger. Make this man a statue or at least name a park bench after him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's well written and feels very grounded which actually adds alot to the creepy factor, I feel it could have had a tiny bit more to really push it over the edge. Not sure what though. Either way I still like as it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …I fucked up

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, is it supposed to be “Special Containment Procedures” like the other articles, or is there a reason this is “Secure Containment Procedures”?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They refers to David Kincaid, as Okoro was never given the designation SCP-4080-1, only PoI-3283. And presumably, this document was written sometime before the end of the re-run period, so there is no telling what will happen once the series hits the end of SCP-4080-1's life again.

From my understanding, this is more like a serialized TV show rather than a weird record of this guy's life for an unknown civilization, whether alien or based on some other sort of spatial/temporal anomaly. When a show has its series finale, that show only repeats old episodes, regardless of the child-series. (Think Breaking Bad to Better Call Saul, or Doctor Who to Torchwood, there are thousands of examples) So maybe other frequencies are broadcasting some sort of spinoff of PoI-3283's life, but it's not something we know about at the time this document was put into the SCP database.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think an interesting angle is that he's called the most important man on Earth, but who is this guy important *to*?

Well, he'd be really important to Achebe Okoro, that's for sure. Maybe everything in David's life was leading up to this one moment, protecting the actual main character of the show.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simple, intriguing, lovely— and an unnerving ending to top it all off. Well done, +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the four second still image makes me incredibly concerned, i donno why, its just so ominous.

item.title: Fastest +50
item.content:encoded:
Thank you all so much for getting this to +50 in under a day! That's the fastest I've ever gotten!

Thank you all so much!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Since SCP-4080-1's death, episodes of SCP-4080 regarding their life have been re-run, starting at the moment they were born.

Who are "they":

  • David Kincaid?
  • Achebe Okoro?

IMO it would be more interesting if protagonist of SCP-4080 series would switch to Achebe Okoro post David Kincaid's death.
If series instead restarted from the beginning of late SCP-4080-1's life, pattern of releasing new episode each day about previous day's events would be broken after (presumably) 68 years.

Each new episode is recorded the day before it is broadcasted and consists of a heavily-edited sequence of events that happened to SCP-4080-1 that day.

Just restarting the series feels… anticlimactic.

-1 for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this. I want to imagine this is just some weird way for the universe to make this relatively simple man happy and feel important, even if he doesn't know it.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My thoughts about this are a little mixed. Right now, though, I think I really love it. It would be easy to dismiss this as a Truman Show ripoff from the pitch alone, but your execution manages to spin it into something totally different, though, more reminiscent of Video Oddity than anything. It's kind of quiet, melancholy, and more than a little eerie, which is plenty enough for me to give it my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is kind of beautiful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay what the fuck. This is really weird and unsettling in the best way possible.

+1 and tell your dad that he's helped make some good shit.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Here's a skip that my Dad helped me make. Hope he's proud of it.

Thanks to Uncle Nicolini, DarkStuff, Captain Kirby, Shaggydredlocks, Dexanote, BananaRepublic, DolphinSlugchugger, Stallmantic, plaguebearer, Jacob Conwell and anyone else who I missed!

Thanks to DolphinSlugChugger for help with Photoshopping the image.

Source is here: https://www.pexels.com/photo/old-man-274849/

Edit: Accidentally just realized that I posted this exactly one year after I posted my first successful skip, so let's pretend that was on purpose
